message:
type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Removing temperature modulation in propose_instruction_for_predictor; adding _cache_salt() method to GroundedProposer; adding cache_salt InputField to DescribeProgram, DescribeModule, and generate_instruction_class's GenerateSingleModuleInstruction signatures; threading cache_salt through GenerateModuleInstruction.forward() and propose_instruction_for_predictor()

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Adding tip_weights param to GroundedProposer.__init__ (new optional dict[str,float]|None arg + self.tip_weights assignment), and modifying propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection block (lines ~360-371, the 'if self.set_tip_randomly:' block) to call new helper _select_tip_with_weights(). Also adding new method _select_tip_with_weights() near propose_instructions_for_program. What construct(s) do you need to touch?

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Confirming no overlap: my changes touch GroundedProposer.__init__ signature (adding tip_weights param + self.tip_weights=... assignment near other self.X= lines), and the tip-selection block inside propose_instructions_for_program (the 'if self.set_tip_randomly:' block, ~lines 360-371) plus a new method _select_tip_with_weights(). Does your cache_salt threading touch __init__ signature/body, or propose_instructions_for_program (not propose_instruction_for_predictor)? Want to make sure our __init__ edits and new-method insertion points don't collide.

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: My exact touch points: (1) new method _cache_salt(self, pred_i, demo_set_i) added to GroundedProposer class, (2) propose_instruction_for_predictor() - remove temperature/epsilon modulation block, add cache_salt computation + pass cache_salt to instruction_generator(...) call, (3) GenerateModuleInstruction.forward() - pass cache_salt param through to describe_program/describe_module/generate_module_instruction calls, (4) DescribeProgram, DescribeModule, generate_instruction_class's GenerateSingleModuleInstruction - add cache_salt InputField. I do NOT touch __init__ or propose_instructions_for_program's tip-selection block. Based on your survey (init + propose_instructions_for_program tip block + new _select_tip_with_weights method), we have NO overlapping constructs. Confirm?

type: AGREE
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Confirmed: no overlapping constructs. You own __init__/tip-selection untouched; I own cache_salt/temperature untouched. Proceeding to implement independently.

type: SURVEY
files: dspy/propose/grounded_proposer.py
note: Confirmed: my changes do NOT touch __init__ signature/body at all, and do NOT touch propose_instructions_for_program's body/tip-selection block. I only touch propose_instruction_for_predictor's body and add a new _cache_salt() method. To avoid insertion collisions: place your _select_tip_with_weights() near/before propose_instructions_for_program (top of class helpers); I'll place _cache_salt() at the END of the GroundedProposer class, after propose_instruction_for_predictor. No overlapping constructs between us - proceeding to implement independently.
